title: Regression in 1.4.x when inserting from a table with missing columns
description:
### What happens?

When INSERTing into one table BY NAME using a SELECT from another table, duckdb >= 1.4.0 fails unless all columns are specified in the SELECT. Older versions required only that the non-nullable columns be specified.

The previous behavior is useful for backwards compatibility: we want to import a saved dataset (from a parquet file) into a destination table and are unsure if the destination has added columns since the last save.

This appears to be a regression in 1.4.0.

### To Reproduce

```
-- Populate source table with a single column
CREATE TABLE source (key INT PRIMARY KEY);
INSERT INTO source VALUES (1);

-- Copy into destination table with an additional nullable column
CREATE TABLE dest (key INT PRIMARY KEY, other INT);
INSERT OR REPLACE INTO dest BY NAME SELECT key FROM source;

-- Check that the row was copied
SELECT * FROM dest;
```

On duckdb <1.4.0 I get:
```
┌───────┬───────┐
│  key  │ other │
│ int32 │ int32 │
├───────┼───────┤
│     1 │  NULL │
└───────┴───────┘
```

On duckdb >= 1.4.0 I get:
```
Binder Error: Values list "excluded" does not have a column named "other"
```

### OS:

Linux x86_64

### DuckDB Version:

1.4.0

### DuckDB Client:

Command Line, Python

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

Daniel Beer

### Affiliation:

Bloomberg LP

### Did you include all relevant configuration (e.g., CPU architecture, Linux distribution) to reproduce the issue?

- [x] Yes, I have

### Did you include all code required to reproduce the issue?

- [x] Yes, I have

### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue?

Yes
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE source (id INT PRIMARY KEY, value VARCHAR(255));
CREATE TABLE dest (id INT PRIMARY KEY, value VARCHAR(255), extra INT DEFAULT 0);
INSERT INTO dest (id, value, extra) SELECT id, NULL, 0 FROM source;
key_bug_features: INSERTing without specifying all columns in SELECT
